title: QQQ ETF Review (2026): Invesco Nasdaq 100 Holdings
description: Complete 2026 review of the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF. Analyze its Nasdaq-100 holdings, performance history, and head-to-head vs SPY and QQQM.

mainEntity.name: What is the QQQ ETF expense ratio?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) has an expense ratio of 0.18%. This means you pay $18 annually for every $10,000 invested.

mainEntity.name: QQQ vs SPY: Which is better?

acceptedAnswer.text: QQQ focuses on the top 100 non-financial Nasdaq companies, making it heavily tech-weighted with higher historical growth and volatility. SPY tracks the broader S&P 500, offering more diversification across all sectors. The better choice depends on your risk tolerance and need for diversification.

mainEntity.name: QQQ vs QQQM: What is the difference?

acceptedAnswer.text: QQQ and QQQM track the exact same Nasdaq-100 index. However, QQQM is designed for buy-and-hold retail investors with a lower expense ratio (0.15% vs 0.18%) and a lower share price. QQQ has vastly higher trading volume, making it better for day traders and options.

Yes, QQQ pays a quarterly dividend, although the yield is typically very low (around 0.29%) because it primarily holds growth-oriented technology companies that reinvest profits rather than paying them out.
QQQ holds the 100 largest non-financial companies listed on the Nasdaq exchange. The top holdings are dominated by mega-cap tech companies such as Apple, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Amazon, and Meta.
